content.full_text:
June 17, 1976

Mr. Frank R. McGoey
Southwestern Portland Cement Company
3055 Wilshire Boulevard
Los Angeles, California 90010

Dear Mr. McGoey:

This letter is to confirm my advice to you by telephone on June 7, 1976, concerning a filing requirement of lobbyist employers under the Political Reform Act.

You asked whether lobbyist employers should allocate and report the compensation of employees, other than lobbyists, who attempt to influence legislative or administrative action. I refer you to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18620(a)(2)(C) and 18620(a)(4), enclosed, which provide that employers of lobbyists must report the proportionate share of gross compensation paid or payable to employees for the portion of compensation time devoted to (1) providing services in support of the lobbyist activities of the lobbyist, (2) directly communicating with elective state officials, legislative officials or agency officials if the purpose of the communication is to influence legislative or administrative action, and (3) developing the material upon which the communication is based or which is transmitted. However, no wages of any employee who spends less than 10 percent of his or her compensated time during any one month providing such services need be allocated or reported.

The allocated portion of compensation should be reported on Form 650, Schedule i. c. o. The filer must state the names and titles of employees to whom $1,000 or more of gross compensation is attributed along with the amount of compensation attributed to each. Attributions of less than $1,000 need only be reported as one lump sum.

If you have further questions regarding your reporting obligations, please contact our staff.

Sincerely,

NAN HAMBLETON
Special Compliance Representative

NH:mak
Enclosures     Jeanne-A ⌐

[The image shows a document on light green paper that appears to be reversed/mirrored and partially illegible due to the quality and orientation. The text is upside down and backwards, making accurate transcription extremely difficult. Most of the content is not clearly readable in this format.]
